---
title: "Rules and Controls"
---

This page explains how to play the browser version of KalahZero and what each
control does. You can read it before playing, or keep it open in another tab.

## Goal

Kalah is a two-player sowing game. The goal is to finish with more stones in
your store than your opponent has in theirs.

In the browser game:

- **South** is the bottom player.
- **North** is the top player.
- Each player owns the six small pits on their side.
- Each player also owns one large store.
- Stones in your store are your score.

## Taking a Turn

On your turn, choose one of your non-empty pits.

The game then:

1. picks up all stones from that pit,
2. drops them one by one into the following pits and your own store,
3. skips your opponent's store,
4. applies any extra-turn or capture rule,
5. passes the move to the next player when appropriate.

Only legal pits are clickable. During animation or while the computer is
thinking, the board waits until the move is finished.

## Extra Turns

You move again if the final stone of your move lands in your own store.

This project also gives you another move when your move captures stones. That
is the rule used by the tutorials and the browser game, so search agents and
human players are playing the same version.

## Captures

A capture happens when your final stone lands in one of your own empty pits and
the opposite pit on your opponent's side contains stones.

When that happens:

- your final stone is captured,
- the stones in the opposite pit are captured,
- all captured stones move into your store,
- you get another move in this project variant.

## Ending the Game

The game ends as soon as one player has no stones left in any of their six
pits.

When that happens, all remaining stones on each side move into that side's own
store. The player with the larger store total wins. If the totals are equal,
the game is a draw.

## Board Display

The score panel shows the current store totals for North and South. The status
line tells you whose turn it is, whether the computer is thinking, or who won
the finished game.

Move labels use pit numbers from the current player's side. For example,
`South 3` means South moved from South's third pit.

Move labels can also include:

- **capture**: the move captured stones,
- **again**: the same player gets another move.

## Controls

**Opponent** chooses the computer player:

- **Greedy** chooses the move that immediately improves its score margin the
  most.
- **Minimax** searches several moves ahead with alpha-beta pruning and a simple
  score-margin heuristic.
- **Random** chooses randomly from legal moves.
- **Trained agent** uses the browser-side trained search agent.

**Depth** appears when Minimax is selected. Move the slider toward **Fast** for
shallower search, or toward **Deep** for stronger but slower play.

**Search** appears when the trained agent is selected. Move the slider toward
**Fast** for quicker responses, or toward **Deep** for more search before the
agent chooses.

**You play as** chooses your side. Changing sides starts a new game.

**Starting stones** chooses how many stones begin in each pit. Changing this
starts a new game.

**Animation speed** changes how quickly stones move around the board. It does
not change the rules or the computer's strength.

**New Game** restarts with the current settings.

## Move History

The move history lists the most recent moves first. Click a move to rewind the
board to the position after that move.

After rewinding, you can continue from that position. If it is the computer's
turn, it will move from the rewound position using the currently selected
opponent.

## Ready to Play

Open [Play Kalah](index.qmd) when you are ready to try a game.
